mnemonics: JOHN Has GROUPed ORDERs BY CLAUSE After WHERE, Having SELECTed FROM many tables
keyPoints: JOIN operations combine rows from two or more tables.
The GROUP BY clause is used with aggregate functions to group the result-set by one or more columns.
The HAVING clause is used to filter records after GROUP BY, because WHERE cannot be used with aggregate functions.
commonMistakes: Using WHERE instead of HAVING to filter aggregated groups.
Forgetting to specify all non-aggregated columns in the GROUP BY clause.
Creating ambiguous joins that result in Cartesian products by forgetting the join condition in the WHERE or ON clause.
